task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves only the ACL column of the AccessControl association identified by InvokingID and MethodID, and returns that ACL as a list of ACE uidrefs. Opal Admin SP and Locking SP preconfigure wildcard descriptor-object rows, MethodID object rows, ACE object rows, and concrete authority/ACE rows. Therefore successful GetACL responses for those remaining issued rows must return the exact ACE list from the row's ACL column: descriptor and MethodID object Gets commonly return ACE_Anybody, Admin Authority_Admin1 Set returns ACE_Set_Enabled, Locking MethodID object Gets return ACE_Anybody, and Locking ACE object Set rows return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression rather than the target ACE's own BooleanExpr authority.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.2.1.4 MethodID (M) The MethodID table is defined in [2], and Table 21 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the MethodID table. *MT1: refer to section 5.1.2 for details on the requirements for supporting Revert. *MT2: refer to section 5.1.1 for details on the requirements for supporting Activate. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.2.1.4 MethodID (M) The MethodID table is defined in [2], and Table 21 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the MethodID table. *MT1: refer to section 5.1.2 for details on the requirements for supporting Revert. *MT2: refer to section 5.1.1 for details on the requirements for supporting Activate. output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

case_name: Admin SP Table_SPInfo descriptor GetACL returns Anybody ACE: impossible return value

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves only the ACL column of the AccessControl association identified by InvokingID and MethodID, and returns that ACL as a list of ACE uidrefs. Opal Admin SP and Locking SP preconfigure wildcard descriptor-object rows, MethodID object rows, ACE object rows, and concrete authority/ACE rows. Therefore successful GetACL responses for those remaining issued rows must return the exact ACE list from the row's ACL column: descriptor and MethodID object Gets commonly return ACE_Anybody, Admin Authority_Admin1 Set returns ACE_Set_Enabled, Locking MethodID object Gets return ACE_Anybody, and Locking ACE object Set rows return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression rather than the target ACE's own BooleanExpr authority.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.2.1.4 MethodID (M) The MethodID table is defined in [2], and Table 21 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the MethodID table. *MT1: refer to section 5.1.2 for details on the requirements for supporting Revert. *MT2: refer to section 5.1.1 for details on the requirements for supporting Activate. Table 21 - Admin SP - MethodID Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 08", "Name": "Next", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 0D", "Name": "GetACL", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 16", "Name": "Get", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 17", "Name": "Set", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 1C", "Name": "Authenticate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 02 *MT1", "Name": "Revert", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 03 *MT2", "Name": "Activate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 06 01", "Name": "Random", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" } ] ```

case_name: Admin SP Table_SPTemplates descriptor GetACL returns Anybody ACE: correct return value

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves only the ACL column of the AccessControl association identified by InvokingID and MethodID, and returns that ACL as a list of ACE uidrefs. Opal Admin SP and Locking SP preconfigure wildcard descriptor-object rows, MethodID object rows, ACE object rows, and concrete authority/ACE rows. Therefore successful GetACL responses for those remaining issued rows must return the exact ACE list from the row's ACL column: descriptor and MethodID object Gets commonly return ACE_Anybody, Admin Authority_Admin1 Set returns ACE_Set_Enabled, Locking MethodID object Gets return ACE_Anybody, and Locking ACE object Set rows return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression rather than the target ACE's own BooleanExpr authority.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.2.1.4 MethodID (M) The MethodID table is defined in [2], and Table 21 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the MethodID table. *MT1: refer to section 5.1.2 for details on the requirements for supporting Revert. *MT2: refer to section 5.1.1 for details on the requirements for supporting Activate. Table 21 - Admin SP - MethodID Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 08", "Name": "Next", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 0D", "Name": "GetACL", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 16", "Name": "Get", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 17", "Name": "Set", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 1C", "Name": "Authenticate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 02 *MT1", "Name": "Revert", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 03 *MT2", "Name": "Activate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 06 01", "Name": "Random", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" } ] ```

case_name: Admin SP Table_SPTemplates descriptor GetACL returns Anybody ACE: impossible return value

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves only the ACL column of the AccessControl association identified by InvokingID and MethodID, and returns that ACL as a list of ACE uidrefs. Opal Admin SP and Locking SP preconfigure wildcard descriptor-object rows, MethodID object rows, ACE object rows, and concrete authority/ACE rows. Therefore successful GetACL responses for those remaining issued rows must return the exact ACE list from the row's ACL column: descriptor and MethodID object Gets commonly return ACE_Anybody, Admin Authority_Admin1 Set returns ACE_Set_Enabled, Locking MethodID object Gets return ACE_Anybody, and Locking ACE object Set rows return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression rather than the target ACE's own BooleanExpr authority.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.2.1.4 MethodID (M) The MethodID table is defined in [2], and Table 21 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the MethodID table. *MT1: refer to section 5.1.2 for details on the requirements for supporting Revert. *MT2: refer to section 5.1.1 for details on the requirements for supporting Activate. Table 21 - Admin SP - MethodID Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 08", "Name": "Next", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 0D", "Name": "GetACL", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 16", "Name": "Get", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 17", "Name": "Set", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 1C", "Name": "Authenticate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 02 *MT1", "Name": "Revert", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 03 *MT2", "Name": "Activate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 06 01", "Name": "Random", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" } ] ```

case_name: Admin SP SPTemplates Base row GetACL returns Anybody ACE: correct return value

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves only the ACL column of the AccessControl association identified by InvokingID and MethodID, and returns that ACL as a list of ACE uidrefs. Opal Admin SP and Locking SP preconfigure wildcard descriptor-object rows, MethodID object rows, ACE object rows, and concrete authority/ACE rows. Therefore successful GetACL responses for those remaining issued rows must return the exact ACE list from the row's ACL column: descriptor and MethodID object Gets commonly return ACE_Anybody, Admin Authority_Admin1 Set returns ACE_Set_Enabled, Locking MethodID object Gets return ACE_Anybody, and Locking ACE object Set rows return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression rather than the target ACE's own BooleanExpr authority.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.2.1.4 MethodID (M) The MethodID table is defined in [2], and Table 21 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the MethodID table. *MT1: refer to section 5.1.2 for details on the requirements for supporting Revert. *MT2: refer to section 5.1.1 for details on the requirements for supporting Activate. Table 21 - Admin SP - MethodID Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 08", "Name": "Next", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 0D", "Name": "GetACL", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 16", "Name": "Get", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 17", "Name": "Set", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 1C", "Name": "Authenticate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 02 *MT1", "Name": "Revert", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 03 *MT2", "Name": "Activate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 06 01", "Name": "Random", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" } ] ```

case_name: Admin SP SPTemplates Base row GetACL returns Anybody ACE: impossible return value

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves only the ACL column of the AccessControl association identified by InvokingID and MethodID, and returns that ACL as a list of ACE uidrefs. Opal Admin SP and Locking SP preconfigure wildcard descriptor-object rows, MethodID object rows, ACE object rows, and concrete authority/ACE rows. Therefore successful GetACL responses for those remaining issued rows must return the exact ACE list from the row's ACL column: descriptor and MethodID object Gets commonly return ACE_Anybody, Admin Authority_Admin1 Set returns ACE_Set_Enabled, Locking MethodID object Gets return ACE_Anybody, and Locking ACE object Set rows return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression rather than the target ACE's own BooleanExpr authority.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.2.1.4 MethodID (M) The MethodID table is defined in [2], and Table 21 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the MethodID table. *MT1: refer to section 5.1.2 for details on the requirements for supporting Revert. *MT2: refer to section 5.1.1 for details on the requirements for supporting Activate. Table 21 - Admin SP - MethodID Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 08", "Name": "Next", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 0D", "Name": "GetACL", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 16", "Name": "Get", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 17", "Name": "Set", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 1C", "Name": "Authenticate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 02 *MT1", "Name": "Revert", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 03 *MT2", "Name": "Activate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 06 01", "Name": "Random", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" } ] ```

output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text

case_name: Admin SP SPTemplates Admin row GetACL returns Anybody ACE: correct return value

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves only the ACL column of the AccessControl association identified by InvokingID and MethodID, and returns that ACL as a list of ACE uidrefs. Opal Admin SP and Locking SP preconfigure wildcard descriptor-object rows, MethodID object rows, ACE object rows, and concrete authority/ACE rows. Therefore successful GetACL responses for those remaining issued rows must return the exact ACE list from the row's ACL column: descriptor and MethodID object Gets commonly return ACE_Anybody, Admin Authority_Admin1 Set returns ACE_Set_Enabled, Locking MethodID object Gets return ACE_Anybody, and Locking ACE object Set rows return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression rather than the target ACE's own BooleanExpr authority.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.2.1.4 MethodID (M) The MethodID table is defined in [2], and Table 21 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the MethodID table. *MT1: refer to section 5.1.2 for details on the requirements for supporting Revert. *MT2: refer to section 5.1.1 for details on the requirements for supporting Activate. Table 21 - Admin SP - MethodID Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 08", "Name": "Next", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 0D", "Name": "GetACL", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 16", "Name": "Get", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 17", "Name": "Set", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 1C", "Name": "Authenticate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 02 *MT1", "Name": "Revert", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 03 *MT2", "Name": "Activate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 06 01", "Name": "Random", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" } ] ```

output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text

case_name: Admin SP SPTemplates Admin row GetACL returns Anybody ACE: impossible return value

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves only the ACL column of the AccessControl association identified by InvokingID and MethodID, and returns that ACL as a list of ACE uidrefs. Opal Admin SP and Locking SP preconfigure wildcard descriptor-object rows, MethodID object rows, ACE object rows, and concrete authority/ACE rows. Therefore successful GetACL responses for those remaining issued rows must return the exact ACE list from the row's ACL column: descriptor and MethodID object Gets commonly return ACE_Anybody, Admin Authority_Admin1 Set returns ACE_Set_Enabled, Locking MethodID object Gets return ACE_Anybody, and Locking ACE object Set rows return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression rather than the target ACE's own BooleanExpr authority.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.2.1.4 MethodID (M) The MethodID table is defined in [2], and Table 21 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the MethodID table. *MT1: refer to section 5.1.2 for details on the requirements for supporting Revert. *MT2: refer to section 5.1.1 for details on the requirements for supporting Activate. Table 21 - Admin SP - MethodID Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 08", "Name": "Next", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 0D", "Name": "GetACL", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 16", "Name": "Get", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 17", "Name": "Set", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 1C", "Name": "Authenticate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 02 *MT1", "Name": "Revert", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 03 *MT2", "Name": "Activate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 06 01", "Name": "Random", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" } ] ```

case_name: Admin SP MethodID GetACL row GetACL returns Anybody ACE: correct return value

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves only the ACL column of the AccessControl association identified by InvokingID and MethodID, and returns that ACL as a list of ACE uidrefs. Opal Admin SP and Locking SP preconfigure wildcard descriptor-object rows, MethodID object rows, ACE object rows, and concrete authority/ACE rows. Therefore successful GetACL responses for those remaining issued rows must return the exact ACE list from the row's ACL column: descriptor and MethodID object Gets commonly return ACE_Anybody, Admin Authority_Admin1 Set returns ACE_Set_Enabled, Locking MethodID object Gets return ACE_Anybody, and Locking ACE object Set rows return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression rather than the target ACE's own BooleanExpr authority.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.2.1.4 MethodID (M) The MethodID table is defined in [2], and Table 21 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the MethodID table. *MT1: refer to section 5.1.2 for details on the requirements for supporting Revert. *MT2: refer to section 5.1.1 for details on the requirements for supporting Activate. Table 21 - Admin SP - MethodID Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 08", "Name": "Next", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 0D", "Name": "GetACL", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 16", "Name": "Get", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 17", "Name": "Set", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 1C", "Name": "Authenticate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 02 *MT1", "Name": "Revert", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 03 *MT2", "Name": "Activate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 06 01", "Name": "Random", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" } ] ```

case_name: Admin SP MethodID GetACL row GetACL returns Anybody ACE: impossible return value

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves only the ACL column of the AccessControl association identified by InvokingID and MethodID, and returns that ACL as a list of ACE uidrefs. Opal Admin SP and Locking SP preconfigure wildcard descriptor-object rows, MethodID object rows, ACE object rows, and concrete authority/ACE rows. Therefore successful GetACL responses for those remaining issued rows must return the exact ACE list from the row's ACL column: descriptor and MethodID object Gets commonly return ACE_Anybody, Admin Authority_Admin1 Set returns ACE_Set_Enabled, Locking MethodID object Gets return ACE_Anybody, and Locking ACE object Set rows return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression rather than the target ACE's own BooleanExpr authority.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.2.1.4 MethodID (M) The MethodID table is defined in [2], and Table 21 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the MethodID table. *MT1: refer to section 5.1.2 for details on the requirements for supporting Revert. *MT2: refer to section 5.1.1 for details on the requirements for supporting Activate. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

case_name: Admin SP ACE Anybody row GetACL returns Anybody ACE: impossible return value

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves only the ACL column of the AccessControl association identified by InvokingID and MethodID, and returns that ACL as a list of ACE uidrefs. Opal Admin SP and Locking SP preconfigure wildcard descriptor-object rows, MethodID object rows, ACE object rows, and concrete authority/ACE rows. Therefore successful GetACL responses for those remaining issued rows must return the exact ACE list from the row's ACL column: descriptor and MethodID object Gets commonly return ACE_Anybody, Admin Authority_Admin1 Set returns ACE_Set_Enabled, Locking MethodID object Gets return ACE_Anybody, and Locking ACE object Set rows return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression rather than the target ACE's own BooleanExpr authority.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.2.1.4 MethodID (M) The MethodID table is defined in [2], and Table 21 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the MethodID table. *MT1: refer to section 5.1.2 for details on the requirements for supporting Revert. *MT2: refer to section 5.1.1 for details on the requirements for supporting Activate. Table 21 - Admin SP - MethodID Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 08", "Name": "Next", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 0D", "Name": "GetACL", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 16", "Name": "Get", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 17", "Name": "Set", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 1C", "Name": "Authenticate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 02 *MT1", "Name": "Revert", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 03 *MT2", "Name": "Activate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 06 01", "Name": "Random", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" } ] ```

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

case_name: Admin SP Authority Admin1 GetACL returns Anybody ACE for Get: correct return value

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: GetACL retrieves only the ACL column of the AccessControl association identified by InvokingID and MethodID, and returns that ACL as a list of ACE uidrefs. Opal Admin SP and Locking SP preconfigure wildcard descriptor-object rows, MethodID object rows, ACE object rows, and concrete authority/ACE rows. Therefore successful GetACL responses for those remaining issued rows must return the exact ACE list from the row's ACL column: descriptor and MethodID object Gets commonly return ACE_Anybody, Admin Authority_Admin1 Set returns ACE_Set_Enabled, Locking MethodID object Gets return ACE_Anybody, and Locking ACE object Set rows return ACE_ACE_Set_BooleanExpression rather than the target ACE's own BooleanExpr authority.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.2.7.5 ACL This is the access control list for this SP/method, table/method, or object/method combination. This column is modified/accessed via the methods GetACL, RemoveACE, and AddACE. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable directly via the Set method.
5.3.2.7.9 GetACLACL This column holds the access control list that controls invocation of the GetACL method on the ACL column this row in the AccessControl table.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.3.1 Result This method returns a list of uidrefs to ACE objects.

5.3.4.3.1 Meta-ACLs The ability to add ACEs to or delete ACEs from ACLs provides granularity of access control, as the authorization required to add or remove ACEs from ACLs MAY be different from the authorization required to invoke the method described in the access control association. The methods that perform the operations that add or remove ACEs from ACLs, or retrieve the list of ACEs from an ACL, are AddACE, RemoveACE, and GetACL. Additionally, access control associations MAY be removed from the AccessControl table through successful invocation of the DeleteMethod method. These four methods together are the meta-ACL methods. Each access control association stores ACLs that govern the authorization requirements for these methods on that access control association. A separate column in the AccessControl table exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the ACL for that meta-ACL method. A separate column in the AccessControl table also exists for each of the meta-ACL methods to store the logging settings for that meta-ACL method. The AccessControl table does not have rows for access control associations representing the meta-ACL methods. In order to add an ACE to an ACL, the ACL for the AddACE method associated with that access control association (stored in the AddACEACL column) SHALL be satisfied. RemoveACE, GetACL, and DeleteMethod function in a similar way, and would have to fulfill the ACL stored in the RemoveACEACL, GetACLACL, and DeleteMethodACL column respectively.

4.2.1.4 MethodID (M) The MethodID table is defined in [2], and Table 21 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the MethodID table. *MT1: refer to section 5.1.2 for details on the requirements for supporting Revert. *MT2: refer to section 5.1.1 for details on the requirements for supporting Activate. Table 21 - Admin SP - MethodID Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 08", "Name": "Next", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 0D", "Name": "GetACL", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 16", "Name": "Get", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 17", "Name": "Set", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 00 1C", "Name": "Authenticate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 02 *MT1", "Name": "Revert", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 02 03 *MT2", "Name": "Activate", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 06 00 00 06 01", "Name": "Random", "CommonName": "", "TemplateID": "" } ] ```
